title: Verilator 4.202 uses Makefile syntax which doesn't work on RHEL/CentOS 7
body:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/commit/6a189a1f8174bfa7c6829f1c0611092c5dbf2bc6, a fix for https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/2834, released in Verilator 4.202, introduced Makefile syntax which is incompatible with GNU Make < 4 shipped with CentOS/RHEL 7, causing `make test` and other builds to fail with an unhelpful error message.

Exemplary error message:

```
make test
# ... snip ...
ar: @Vt_a1_first_cc__ALL.a.tmp: No such file or directory
```

The `.tmp` file is created through code in `verilated.mk`:

```
	$(file >$@.tmp)
	$(foreach L, $(filter-out %.a,$^), $(file >>$@.tmp, $L))
```

Running with `make --warn-undefined-variables test` results in 

```
/home/abuild/rpmbuild/BUILD/verilator-4.202/test_regress/../include/verilated.mk:210: warning: undefined variable `file >Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.a.tmp'
/home/abuild/rpmbuild/BUILD/verilator-4.202/test_regress/../include/verilated.mk:210: warning: undefined variable `file >>Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.a.tmp, Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.o'
```

The [`file` make function](https://www.gnu.org/software/make/manual/html_node/File-Function.html) seems to be added in 2012 and was first released in GNU Make 4.0 (https://github.com/ndmitchell/gnumake/blob/master/ChangeLog#L234). RHEL/CentOS7 ships with GNU Make 3.82.
code_blocks:
/home/abuild/rpmbuild/BUILD/verilator-4.202/test_regress/../include/verilated.mk:210: warning: undefined variable `file >Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.a.tmp'
/home/abuild/rpmbuild/BUILD/verilator-4.202/test_regress/../include/verilated.mk:210: warning: undefined variable `file >>Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.a.tmp, Vt_EXAMPLE__ALL.o'
